commit.message:
8378168

Hi all,

  please review this change that fixes a potential issue with reclaiming empty regions during remark - pinned regions should not be for obvious reasons, but the code in the pause actually never flushes the pin count caches of the threads. This means that the count may not be accurated and we may reclaim that region incorrectly, causing data corruption and crashes.

There is no known issue attached to that, found while refactoring in this area.

A similar issue presents itself with filling the heap with filler objects - we can't, in debug builds, zap the payloads of the filler objects if the region is pinned. However depending on the caller the pin count of the region may also not be accurate for the same reasons.
Instead of trying to figure out automatically if a region's pin count is correct, let the caller decide to allow zapping.

Again, there is no known issue attached to that, found while refactoring in this area.

Afaict other code that writes dummy/filler object is fine as they all write into unallocated areas (which can't contain a pinned object by definition).

Testing: gha

Thanks,
  Thomas
